demographics.living_situation: resides in a residential aged care facility

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My left leg is completely dead and freezing, and the pain in my calf is unbearable.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Sudden severe leg pain with numbness and coldness that started a couple of hours ago, reported by facility staff on my behalf.

symptom_narrative.duration: constant and worsening since it started
symptom_narrative.character: excruciating, unremitting pain in the calf and foot
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, not relieved by rest or the facility's usual pain medicine
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing has helped, including the usual pain tablets
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: toes went numb first and now my whole foot has no feeling, and I can't wiggle my toes at all
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I do get cramping in my legs when I walk with my walker (known claudication), but nothing like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: usually get around with a walker, now can't move the leg at all and staff have me in bed

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: poor circulation in my legs, cramping when I walk

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in residential aged care with nursing staff present and assisting with this consultation

objective_data_offered.value: foot and lower leg look very pale with early blotchy patches, visible on video shown by nursing staff

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: frightened, in severe pain, relying on facility staff to communicate on her behalf

communication_style.typical_message_style: brief statements due to pain; nursing staff relay most details and observations

access_barriers.transport: resides in aged care facility; ambulance transfer required, facility staff coordinate handover
